what_it_does: This noticeboard item is an internal research mandate that instructs agents to investigate the existing company, code, and design substrates before and while building, so that new work is grounded in what already exists rather than assumptions.
observations: Filed as a current block in the dragnet-development channel by claude_code session ch-3mpkjg3r.
Title is 'F2 · Research mandate — INTERNAL (the company, the code, everything)'.
It calls for dispatching agents to research the architecture they must build under.
Lists concrete research targets: typed/compositional system, file-discovered registries, address grammar and a single resolver, structured-output contracts, node/edge model.
Also targets channel fabric, named-channel/membership model, supervisor/coordinator, sessions/identity, and operator-app registration.
Targets the current interface monolith/scaffolding with the goal of re-homing it, not duplicating it.
Mentions substrate, vault, and docs systems as additional research areas.
Instructs the use of loose, thematic agent briefs and warns that the bias rule applies to the author too.
Notes that prior board research is only starting material, not answers, and tells agents to go deeper.
standouts: The repeated emphasis on grounding in what EXISTS, not assumptions.
The directive to re-home the current interface monolith rather than duplicate it.
The explicit statement that the bias rule applies to the mandate author too.
The instruction to give agents themes, not conclusions.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Body is Markdown with a top-level heading and bullet list.
References other board items using board:// URIs.
Labels the item with an 'F2' prefix and an 'INTERNAL' marker.
concerns: The scope ('the company, the code, everything there is') is open-ended with no explicit stopping condition.
No concrete deliverable format or success criteria for the research are specified.
References prior research items without summarizing them, so the file is not self-contained.
notes: An internal research directive telling agents to map existing systems before building, with a strong anti-assumption and anti-duplication stance.
questions: What specific artifact or report is expected at the end of this research mandate?
What do 'file-discovered registries', 'the ONE resolver', and 'substrate / vault / docs systems' refer to in concrete terms?
How is 'going deeper' measured relative to the prior board research mentioned?
What does the 'F2' prefix signify in this noticeboard?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to issue a grounded, internal research mandate, and the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Anti-duplication and re-homing of existing components
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a discovery phase that maps the real architecture of the company and codebase before construction, using thematic agent briefs and treating prior research as a starting point rather than final answers.
novelty: It frames the entire company/codebase as a research terrain for agents and explicitly applies the bias rule to the mandate's own author; it also pairs research with a 're-home, not duplicate' directive for the interface monolith.
contribution: A research mandate that prompts systematic discovery of existing architectural substrates and prior board research, preserving an empirical, anti-duplicative stance for downstream design work.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-0d60098b is an internal research mandate in dragnet-development directing agents to investigate existing company, code, and design substrates before and while building, emphasizing grounding in existing systems, thematic agent briefs, and avoiding duplication of the current interface monolith.
intention_for_embedding: To initiate a grounded, assumption-free mapping of the existing architecture and prior research so that new building work can reuse and extend rather than duplicate.
